name: American Samoa (United States)

colors.symbolism: loyalty; a traditional Samoan colour and the colour shared with the United States flag

description: A blue field crossed by a white triangle that points from the fly side toward the hoist, edged by red bands; the white triangle is charged with a bald eagle clutching a Samoan uatogi (war club) and fue (fly-whisk).
history: Adopted on 24 April 1960 to replace the United States flag as the territorial flag. The design was the work of a committee that included Samoan chiefs and was intended to combine American and Samoan symbols. American Samoa is an unincorporated, unorganised territory of the United States.

features.symbolism: a white pile fimbriated red runs from the fly edge toward the hoist, leaving dark blue triangles above and below; the red bands frame the white wedge

features.role: American bald eagle clutching a Samoan uatogi (war club) and fue (fly-whisk)
features.symbolism: the eagle represents the United States, while the uatogi and fue are traditional symbols of the authority of Samoan chiefs (matai) — together expressing the territory's relationship with the United States and respect for Samoan custom
